misconceptions.description: Tries a u = sin x substitution on an even power like sin^2 x (which has no cosine factor to become du), or peels the wrong function on an odd power. The strategy depends on which power is odd.
trigger_phrases: let u = sin x for sin^2

misconceptions.display_name: Integrates sin^2 as if it were a simple power
misconceptions.description: Writes integral sin^2 x dx as sin^3 x/3 or -cos^2 x/2, treating sin^2 x like a power-rule integrand instead of using the power-reduction identity sin^2 x = (1 - cos 2x)/2.
Integrates cos(2x) to sin(2x) instead of sin(2x)/2, forgetting the reciprocal of the inner coefficient. integral cos(kx) dx = sin(kx)/k.
